expectations.text: Cargo.toml includes yauth with email-password, passkey, and mfa features
expectations.evidence: Includes yauth but ALSO adds yauth-entity and yauth-migration as separate crates (lines 24-25). These don't exist as separate published crates — entity and migration are internal modules of the yauth crate.

expectations.text: Code creates a database pool and runs migrations
expectations.evidence: Uses fabricated API: yauth::create_pool(&config) with config struct (line 81) — actual API is yauth::create_pool(&database_url, &yauth_config). Migration call uses yauth_migration::run_migrations (line 84) — actual API is yauth::migration::diesel_migrations::run_migrations(&pool).

expectations.text: YAuthBuilder is configured with EmailPasswordConfig, PasskeyConfig, and MfaConfig
expectations.evidence: Builder pattern is correct with all three plugins configured (lines 89-116)

expectations.text: Auth routes are nested under /api/auth using .nest()
A protected /api/me route uses auth_middleware and extracts Extension<AuthUser>
expectations.evidence: Uses Extension<AuthUser> correctly but does NOT apply auth_middleware — just nests the route under /api without middleware layer (line 126-144). Also imports AuthUser from wrong path: yauth::{AuthUser, YAuthBuilder, YAuthConfig} (line 48) vs actual yauth::middleware::AuthUser.

expectations.text: YAuthConfig includes base_url, session settings, and trusted_origins
expectations.evidence: Uses fabricated config: YAuthConfig { database_url: ... } (line 74) — actual config doesn't have database_url field. Missing base_url, session_cookie_name, session_ttl, trusted_origins fields.
